pages.raw_text:
Special Correspondence to The Press


BOISE, Idaho, April 13.-Signs
are now clear enough. if ever there
was doubt, that this spot will soon
be a center of world interest. The
first trial in the celebrated case
growing out Of the murder Of for
mer Governor Frank Steunenberg
has been set for May 9, when VIM.
d. Haywood. secretary of the West
ern Federation of Miners, will be
arraigned.


While the murder Of Steuenberg
was committed in the little town
Of Caldwell, Canyon county, this
city has been headquarters for all
those concerned in the case. Meyer,
Haywood and Pettibone were trans
ferred to the Ada county Jail here
because it offered more comfort
able quarters than could be afford
ed by the Jail at Caldwell.


Governor Frank r. Gooding, who
has had charge of the prosecution
on behalf Of the state, is here. At
torney j. Fl. Hawley. leading coun-
sci for the state. and his associate.
Senator w. E. Borah. one of the
greatest legal lights in this section
of the country. are members of the
capital city bar.


Attorney J. II. Nugent, who has
been in active charge Of the
preparation of the case for the de-
Tense, established an Office here
soon after the arrest of the prison
ers, and has spent much of his
time in this city.


Also, and of large Importance, is
the fact that Boise has been head
quarters for the detectives en-
